summary: Add the principal Upanishads (Müller SBE Vols 1 & 15) — ~174 per-page entries under tradition=upanishads
description:
## Scope

Add the principal Upanishads to the corpus from Müller's translations in Sacred Books of the East Vols. 1 and 15. Tradition slug: `upanishads` (chosen over `vedanta` — see `docs/corpus-expansion/url-vetting.md` §5.1). Roughly 174 per-page sub-entries to be **script-generated** from the SBE volume-index files (`sacred-texts.com/hin/sbe15/index.htm`, `sacred-texts.com/hin/sbe01/index.htm`), NOT authored by hand. Each entry gets a matching `chunking/upanishads/{id}.toml`; configs are highly uniform so script-generate those too.

## Per-Upanishad scope (user-selected pragmatic prune)

- Katha (6 pp), Mundaka (6 pp), Svetâsvatara (6 pp), Prasña (6 pp), Kena (4 pp): **full**
- Maitrâyana (7 pp): **full**
- Taittirîya (31 pp): **full**
- Brihadâranyaka (46 pp): **full except skip sbe15098** (Hume-translation insert in the Müller sequence)
- Aitareya: **scope-prune to II.4–7 only** (the Upanishad proper, 6 pp; defer the wider Aitareya-Âranyaka 59-page apparatus)
- Chandogya/Khândogya: **scope-prune to Prapâthakas VI–VIII** (55 pp; covers tat tvam asi, honey-doctrine, Indra-Virocana, Sândilya-vidyâ; defer I–V to a later expansion)
- Îsâ: single page sbe01243 — needs per-config `pre_strip_patterns` to drop Müller's inline verse-by-verse commentary tail

Per-Upanishad file maps: `docs/corpus-expansion/url-vetting.md` §3.4 (Brihadâranyaka) and §3.5 (Chandogya). Other Upanishads' ranges in §3.2.

## Per-config pre_strip

Each chunking config also strips the trailing Footnotes apparatus block on its SBE page. Sample regex tested against the existing committed Yasna raw output:

    pre_strip_patterns = ['(?m)^Footnotes\b[\s\S]*$']

Rationale: `docs/corpus-expansion/sbe-downloader-patch-assessment.md` §3.3 — the downloader doesn't strip this block today, the per-config layer does. Yasna precedent: `sources/manifest.toml:386-440`.

## Pipeline

acquire → chunk → embed_corpus → re-tag → re-edges. Standard chunk_id namespace: `upanishads.{id}.NNN`.

## Out of scope

- Mandukya-Upanishad: not in Müller's SBE. Needs Hume 1921 from archive.org — separate ticket later.
- SBE downloader patch (inline `<font size="1">` strip): separate quality ticket. Pipeline tolerates the artifacts today; corpus already runs with same artifacts in Yasnas.

## Files

- `docs/corpus-expansion/extra.md` — draft manifest, all 11 URLs verified 2026-05-31
- `docs/corpus-expansion/url-vetting.md` — vetting report with per-Upanishad file maps
- `docs/corpus-expansion/sbe-downloader-patch-assessment.md` — per-config pre_strip rationale
- `sources/manifest.toml` — append target
